module.name: src
module.purpose: Rust workspace source root for Marix crates with flat Cargo crate layout. It organizes reusable common config/external re-export/logging/protocol/structure modules (protocol now owns the ToolSchema and ToolPreview tool metadata contracts), agent model/session/task orchestration, client session/event surfaces, host-side tool execution with an implemented per-execution runtime that runs the Tool handle from the marix-host-tool crate, and the host/tool crate (marix-host-tool) that exposes the Executor-side Tool handle and the ToolProgram interface and builds one executable per native tool, while Cargo manifests define crate wiring.

childModules.name: agent
childModules.purpose: Agent crate namespace for model backend abstractions, Session and SessionContext runtime orchestration, and the implemented task runtime. The model backend abstraction exposes only a send interface bridged by a blanket impl and includes an implemented Deepseek streaming backend; the task module implements Task, which drives model steps and streams status to the client via synchronous try_send, backed by the shared TaskContext state that holds the std::sync::Mutex-guarded model backend and client/host senders.

childModules.name: client
childModules.purpose: Client crate namespace for user-facing session commands, client events, and channel endpoints that communicate with the agent through shared SessionEvent values.

childModules.name: common
childModules.purpose: Shared common crate namespace with reusable config, now-public crate-local external re-export adapters that let downstream crates route third-party access through common, logging, protocol models including the ToolSchema and ToolPreview tool metadata contracts, and structural data/channel interfaces.

childModules.name: host
childModules.purpose: Host crate namespace for agent channel connection stubs and host-side tool execution. The executor folder module's Executor tracks ExecutionRuntime records in a WorkQueue keyed by ExeId, each backed by a shared ExecutionContext, and exposes preview, evoke, query, cancel, kill, and update interfaces; the execution child module now implements ExecutionRuntime, which runs a Tool (imported from the marix-host-tool crate that host/Cargo.toml now depends on) via ExecutionContext and streams ExecutionStatus/ExecutionUpdate to the agent by synchronously locking agent_tx (std::sync::Mutex) and calling try_send. The host/tool folder is the standalone marix-host-tool crate that exposes the Executor-side Tool handle and the ToolProgram interface and compiles one executable per native tool (read, write, list, search, shell).
